SoC Monitoring.title: Close colorectal cancer screening gap
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Highest routine maintenance priority because age-eligible colorectal screening status is undocumented and may be due now; result determines a long-term cancer screening cadence.

SoC Monitoring.title: Hereditary cancer genetics visit to operationalize ATM surveillance
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Known pathogenic ATM variant needs a documented surveillance and cascade-testing plan so downstream organ-specific monitoring is correctly applied and overuse is avoided.

SoC Monitoring.title: Determine pancreatic surveillance eligibility without defaulting to imaging
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: ATM status plus unclear GI family history creates a surveillance eligibility question; gating before imaging prevents both missed high-risk pathways and premature MRI/EUS.

SoC Monitoring.title: Reconcile paternal grandfather GI cancer primary site
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Family-history clarification directly affects cancer surveillance eligibility and intervals, especially pancreatic or colorectal pathways.

SoC Monitoring.title: Establish PSA-based prostate screening baseline through shared decision-making
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Age, male sex, missing PSA history, and inherited-risk context make PSA discussion/baseline documentation a timely preventive maintenance item.

SoC Monitoring.title: Reconcile immunization history and close age-appropriate preventive schedule
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: All vaccine records are missing, so routine adult preventive schedule closure cannot be determined until history is reconciled.

SoC Monitoring.title: Perform orthostatic vital-sign assessment for rare lightheadedness
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Symptom-confirmed presyncope-like episodes merit a focused orthostatic baseline and red-flag screen despite low frequency and athletic context.

SoC Monitoring.title: Monitor chronic low back and hip/glute tendon symptoms for red flags and functional decline
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Persistent musculoskeletal symptoms require red-flag and function surveillance while avoiding premature imaging if uncomplicated.

SoC Monitoring.title: Clarify gout diagnosis and monitor urate pathway
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Prior recurrent podagra-like attacks were clinically meaningful; diagnosis, urate burden, stone history, and recurrence status need documentation even though symptoms are currently quiet.

SoC Monitoring.title: Standardize renal marker follow-up for creatinine-cystatin C discordance
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Discordant renal markers in a high-fitness phenotype should be trended under standardized conditions to avoid CKD mislabeling and detect true renal change.

SoC Monitoring.title: Reconcile magnesium and supplement use for safety monitoring
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Structured supplement list conflicts with narrative magnesium use; reconciliation supports renal/electrolyte safety and correct interpretation of gout-like symptom history.

SoC Monitoring.title: Set blood pressure surveillance cadence and home-monitoring trigger
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Clinic BP is normal, so routine cadence and triggers for home monitoring are appropriate rather than intensive monitoring now.

SoC Monitoring.title: Set lipid and ApoB monitoring cadence
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Borderline LDL-C/ApoB need longitudinal preventive tracking, but CAC=0 and otherwise favorable markers make this routine rather than urgent.

SoC Monitoring.title: Document CAC=0 and avoid premature repeat CAC scanning
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Current CAC=0 should be recorded and used to prevent unnecessary near-term repeat radiation-based imaging.

SoC Monitoring.title: Continue periodic diabetes and metabolic screening
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Current glycemic markers are normal; age and ancestry support routine periodic screening rather than near-term escalation.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Receive annual influenza vaccination if not already current
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Annual influenza vaccination is a high-evidence, low-burden adult risk-reduction action when not current; missing records make status uncertain but easy to close.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Stay current with COVID-19 vaccination if not already current
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: If not up to date, vaccination meaningfully reduces severe COVID-19 risk with strong evidence and low implementation burden.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Preserve regular aerobic activity as a cardiometabolic and neurovascular risk-reduction lever
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Maintaining existing high aerobic activity protects against major cardiometabolic outcomes; relevance remains high even though this is mostly trajectory preservation rather than new initiation.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Complete hepatitis B vaccination if unvaccinated or nonimmune
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: HBV vaccination is guideline-grade adult infection and liver-disease prevention; current immunity/vaccine status is missing, so action depends on reconciliation.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Use graded load management and rehabilitation for chronic low back and tendon insertion symptoms
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Current persistent tendon/back symptoms make relevance high, and standard graded rehab can redirect the course before chronic limitation or injury progression.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Maintain healthy weight and avoid central adiposity to reduce diabetes and cardiometabolic risk
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Diabetes and cardiometabolic outcomes are important, especially with age and East Asian ancestry; current status is favorable, so the action is maintenance of a protective trajectory.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Use kidney-safety precautions during endurance activity, illness, and pain episodes
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: AKI prevention during dehydration, illness, endurance activity, or NSAID use has strong plausibility and practical impact without labeling the patient as having CKD.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Use sport-specific protective equipment and return-to-play precautions
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: High-injury-risk sports plus current musculoskeletal symptoms make preventive gear, technique, and return-to-play precautions relevant and potentially trajectory-preserving.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Adopt or maintain a lipid-lowering dietary pattern for LDL-C and ApoB reduction
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: LDL-C/ApoB are measured modifiable risk drivers; diet is evidence-based and appropriate now, though absolute near-term risk is tempered by CAC=0 and favorable physiology.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Use guideline-threshold statin pharmacotherapy only if standard cardiovascular eligibility is met
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Statins have strong evidence when eligibility is met, but this patient’s current CAC=0 and low-strain profile make immediate medication relevance lower and threshold-dependent.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Maintain blood-pressure-protective diet and sodium pattern despite currently normal clinic BP
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Hypertension prevention has high outcome importance and strong evidence, but current BP is normal and orthostatic symptoms argue against extreme sodium restriction.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Maintain consistent sleep duration and recovery hygiene
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Sleep supports cardiometabolic and recovery outcomes; current sleep is already favorable, so this is maintenance with moderate incremental impact.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Keep alcohol intake within low-risk limits and avoid binge drinking
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Reported intake is already low, but maintaining low-risk use and avoiding binges supports gout, BP, cancer, sleep, and injury prevention with little burden.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Use standard gout-recurrence prevention if gout history is confirmed
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: If gout is confirmed, recurrence prevention and urate-lowering thresholds are standard; current flares are absent and diagnosis is unconfirmed, so action remains conditional.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Use orthostatic safety behaviors to reduce presyncope-related injury risk
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Episodes are rare, lowering probability, but gradual standing, hydration awareness, and countermeasures are easy and can prevent fall or presyncope injury.

Adjunct Options.title: Video-based movement mechanics review
Adjunct Options.rationale: Addresses the patient’s movement-retraining hypothesis and could redirect mechanical load, but quality depends on reviewer skill and is adjunctive to clinical assessment.

Adjunct Options.title: Tendon 24-hour load-response log
Adjunct Options.rationale: Directly matches current tendon symptoms, is low-cost and low-risk, and can clarify training triggers, though evidence is mainly practical rather than disease-outcome grade.

Adjunct Options.title: Wearable-guided deload rule for tendon recovery
Adjunct Options.rationale: Combines current symptoms with existing wearable data to anchor recovery decisions, but device variability and anxiety risk limit evidence quality.

Adjunct Options.title: Counterpressure maneuvers for rare standing lightheadedness
Adjunct Options.rationale: Very easy and low-risk for rare orthostatic symptoms, with plausible human analog support, but low event frequency limits expected benefit.

Adjunct Options.title: HRV/RHR illness and recovery early-warning rule
Adjunct Options.rationale: Patient already has recovery data, making implementation easy; benefit is mainly training and illness self-management because wearable metrics are nonspecific.

Adjunct Options.title: Hydration-electrolyte pre-load for orthostatic-prone situations
Adjunct Options.rationale: Targeted use before heat, long rides, or prolonged standing is plausible and practical, but should not become indiscriminate salt/fluid loading.

Adjunct Options.title: Bike and sport equipment fit check
Adjunct Options.rationale: Reasonable if symptoms correlate with MTB or sport gear; potential mechanical benefit is offset by cost and uncertain causality.

Adjunct Options.title: Magnesium N-of-1 continuation log
Adjunct Options.rationale: Preserves a patient-observed gout-like symptom signal with safety tracking, but magnesium is not established gout prophylaxis and dose/status are unknown.

Adjunct Options.title: Elective imaging radiation-minimization preference
Adjunct Options.rationale: ATM status makes an elective radiation-minimization preference plausible and easy, but outcome evidence is uncertain and it must not delay indicated imaging.

Adjunct Options.title: Alcohol and purine trigger self-experiment
Adjunct Options.rationale: Useful only if flares recur; it may identify triggers with low burden but could become unnecessarily restrictive given unconfirmed, currently quiescent gout.

Adjunct Options.title: Optional sex or couples counseling for desire discrepancy
Adjunct Options.rationale: Directly matches a quality-of-life narrative signal and may help if desired, but requires patient and possibly partner readiness and is not a biologic disease intervention.

Adjunct Options.title: Collagen or gelatin plus vitamin C tendon-support trial
Adjunct Options.rationale: Mechanistically coherent for tendon symptoms with modest burden, but clinical effect size and supplement quality are uncertain.

Adjunct Options.title: Protein distribution experiment for recovery
Adjunct Options.rationale: May support recovery in high training load, but no protein deficit is documented, so incremental benefit is uncertain.

Adjunct Options.title: Sleep timing and light-exposure recovery experiment
Adjunct Options.rationale: Circadian regularity is plausible and low-risk, but sleep duration/efficiency already appear favorable, making it optimization rather than correction.

Adjunct Options.title: Tart cherry or anthocyanin food trial for gout-like recurrence
Adjunct Options.rationale: Low-downside if food-first and flares recur, but current symptoms are absent, gout is unconfirmed, and evidence is not standard-care grade.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: Low-dose CT lung cancer screening
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: Patient is a never smoker with 0 pack-years; LDCT is marked not applicable.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: Routine mammography or breast MRI
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: Male sex at birth and breast screening fields are marked not applicable; no breast symptom signal supplied.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: Pap smear or high-risk HPV cervical screening
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: Male sex at birth; Pap/HPV screening marked not applicable.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: Treat Lp(a) as elevated based on old or converted value
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: Authoritative current Lp(a) is 72 nmol/L and flagged optimal; old 62 mg/dL value is superseded.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: Immediate repeat CAC scan
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: CAC was completed in 2026 with score 0; immediate repeat would not add coherent surveillance value.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: Screening CCTA or carotid imaging for plaque hunting
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: Asymptomatic patient with CAC=0 and low-strain cardiometabolic profile; no supplied trigger for advanced vascular imaging.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: Aggressive lipid drug escalation solely from invalid Lp(a) or high-risk framing
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: Current Lp(a) is not elevated and CAC=0 argues against high near-term plaque-burden framing.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: Diagnose chronic kidney disease from creatinine alone
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: Creatinine eGFR is lower, but cystatin C eGFR, UACR, urinalysis, and BP are reassuring in a high-fitness phenotype.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: Start urate-lowering therapy now
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: Gout-like attacks are historical and reportedly resolved; serum uric acid, stone history, and clinician diagnosis are missing.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: Treat magnesium as proven gout prevention
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: Patient-reported improvement after magnesium is useful to document but does not establish magnesium as definitive gout therapy.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: Label rare orthostatic symptoms as established dysautonomia or POTS
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: Episodes are rare and no syncope, palpitations, chest pain, exertional symptoms, abnormal rhythm alert, or orthostatic vitals are supplied.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: Brain MRI, amyloid PET, or Alzheimer biomarker screening
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: No cognitive symptom signal or neuro red flag is supplied; neuro imaging is marked not standard onboarding.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: Testosterone therapy for low libido
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: Patient explicitly reports preserved high sex drive and total testosterone is normal in supplied labs.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: H. pylori eradication or viral hepatitis treatment for cancer prevention
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: H. pylori, HBsAg, and HCV antibody are negative; no infection treatment target is supplied.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: Immediate spine/hip MRI or broad rheumatology panel for tendon/back symptoms
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: Symptoms are chronic but no neurologic, cancer, infection, inflammatory arthritis, or severe functional red flags were supplied.

input_sufficiency.notes: Completed ranking used the supplied candidate artifact without creating or renaming candidates. Key limitations affecting confidence include absent mandatory-action register, no prior colorectal/PSA/immunization records, incomplete family cancer primary-site details, no formal ASCVD risk calculation, no serum uric acid or confirmed gout diagnosis, no orthostatic vitals/home BP stream, no detailed diet/protein/NSAID/supplement history, and a conflict between empty structured supplement list and narrative magnesium use.

spareq_definitions.P: probability/relevance of that outcome for this patient
